title: Phase 1 spec: plugins + skills (loader, marketplace, MCP/LSP integration, autoupdate, blocklist) [CC parity]
description:
## Goal

Extract a behavioral spec for this subsystem by reading Claude Code's TypeScript source. **Output**: update this issue body with a markdown spec pinning down each testable contract (input → expected output, edge cases, error paths). No Rust yet — that is Phase 2.

Parent audit: #523.

## Claude Code source — read these

- `utils/plugins/pluginLoader.ts` (~3302 lines)
- `utils/plugins/marketplaceManager.ts` (~2643 lines)
- `utils/plugins/installedPluginsManager.ts` (~1268 lines)
- `utils/plugins/mcpPluginIntegration.ts` (~634 lines)
- `utils/plugins/lspPluginIntegration.ts` (~387 lines)
- `utils/plugins/pluginAutoupdate.ts` (~284 lines)
- `utils/plugins/pluginBlocklist.ts` (~127 lines)
- `skills/bundled/scheduleRemoteAgents.ts` (~447 lines)

## OpenClaudia counterpart

- `src/plugins/manager.rs` (~885 lines) — strict_known_marketplaces 315
- `src/plugins/marketplace.rs` (~158 lines)
- `src/plugins/install.rs` (~192 lines)
- `src/plugins/policy.rs` (~331 lines)
- `src/skills.rs` (~90 lines) — parse_skill_file 37-54, load_skills 63-71

---

## Behavioral spec

### B1 — PluginManager discovery: marketplace cache vs. local plugin dirs

**CC reference:** `pluginLoader.ts` lines 1–33 (module doc), `pluginDirectories.ts` `getPluginsDirectory()` returns `~/.claude/plugins/`; marketplace entries come from `~/.claude/plugins/known_marketplaces.json` (marketplaceManager.ts:103). Plugin cache is under `~/.claude/plugins/cache/<marketplace>/<plugin>/<version>/` (pluginLoader.ts:143–161). Session-only plugins are injected from `--plugin-dir` CLI flag without going through the cache (pluginLoader.ts comment: "Plugin Discovery Sources").

**OC reference:** `src/plugins/manager.rs:29–47` (`PluginManager::new`) scans `~/.openclaudia/plugins/`, `~/.claude/plugins/` (compatibility shim), and `.openclaudia/plugins/`. Installed plugins are also read from `~/.openclaudia/plugins/installed_plugins.json` (V2 format, manager.rs:107–143).

**Contract:**
- Input: filesystem at startup, `installed_plugins.json` V2 on disk.
- Expected output: every directory under each search path that has `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` (CC format), `plugin.json` at root, or legacy `manifest.json` is loaded as a `Plugin`. Directories with none of these are silently skipped (`ManifestNotFound` is absorbed, not surfaced — manager.rs:94).
- Installed-plugins path (`installed_plugins.json`) is consulted second; if a plugin by the same name is already loaded from a search path, the installed entry is skipped (manager.rs:116–119).
- If `installed_plugins.json` is missing or unparseable, the manager starts with an empty installed map (no panic, no error propagation — install.rs:116–135).
- Edge case: a search path directory that does not exist is silently skipped with a `debug!` log (manager.rs:65–67); failure to `read_dir` on an existing path emits `warn!` and continues (manager.rs:70–74).
- **CC gap:** CC uses a versioned cache path `~/.claude/plugins/cache/<mp>/<plugin>/<ver>/` with seed-dir probing and zip-cache fallback (pluginLoader.ts:139–210). OC scans raw search paths only — no versioned cache layout, no seed-dir probing. (Gap: see filed issue #AUTOUPDATE-ISSUE, #ZIPCACHE-ISSUE.)

---

### B2 — Plugin blocked by policy returns `PluginError::PolicyRejected`

**CC reference:** `marketplaceHelpers.ts:480–496` `isSourceAllowedByPolicy`: checks `blockedMarketplaces` first (returns `false`); then checks `strictKnownMarketplaces` allowlist (if set and no match, returns `false`). Rejection happens before the filesystem is touched (pluginLoader.ts: "check happens before touching the filesystem").

**OC reference:** `src/plugins/policy.rs:75–93` `check_marketplace_allowed`. `src/plugins/manager.rs:305–323` `policy_rejection_to_error` maps `PolicyRejection::Blocked` → reason `"source is on the block list"` and `PolicyRejection::NotInAllowlist` → reason `"source is not on the allowed list (strict_known_marketplaces)"`. Result is `PluginError::PolicyRejected { reason, scope }` where scope is `"managed"` or `"user"` (manager.rs:319–321).

**Contract:**
- Input: `(source: MarketplaceSource, policy: PluginPolicy)`.
- **Blocklist takes precedence**: if source matches any `blocked_marketplaces` entry, result is `Err(PolicyRejected { reason: "source is on the block list", scope })` — even if the source also appears in the allowlist (policy.rs:79–84, test at policy.rs:236–246).
- **Allowlist enforcement**: if `strict_known_marketplaces` is `Some(list)` and no entry matches, result is `Err(PolicyRejected { reason: "source is not on the allowed list …", scope })`.
- **No allowlist** (`None`): any source is permitted; blocklist still applies (policy.rs:86).
- **Empty allowlist** (`Some([])`): every source is rejected with `NotInAllowlist` (policy.rs test line 222–231).
- Edge case — GitHub matching is case-insensitive on `repo`; `ref` and `path` on the rule side `None` = wildcard (policy.rs:135–138, 173–178).
- Edge case — Git URLs are canonicalized by stripping trailing `.git` before comparison; `http://` vs `https://` are NOT treated as equal (policy.rs:185–187).
- Edge case — Mixed variants (GitHub rule vs. Git candidate) never match; prevents bypassing a GitHub-only allowlist via a raw git URL (policy.rs:259–272).
- Side effect: rejection is logged at `warn` level for managed policies, `debug` for user policies (policy.rs:33 doc comment).

---

### B3 — `strict_known_marketplaces` rejects sources not in allowlist

**CC reference:** `marketplaceHelpers.ts:159–165` `getStrictKnownMarketplaces()` reads `policySettings.strictKnownMarketplaces`; `isSourceAllowedByPolicy` (line 480–496) checks allowlist second (after blocklist). A missing field (`null`/`undefined`) means no enforcement; an empty array means nothing is allowed.

**OC reference:** `src/plugins/policy.rs:38–39` `strict_known_marketplaces: Option<Vec<MarketplaceSource>>`. Semantics: `None` = no enforcement; `Some([])` = nothing allowed; `Some([…])` = only listed sources allowed. `check_marketplace_allowed` (policy.rs:75–93) implements the two-phase check.

**Contract:**
- `PluginPolicy { strict_known_marketplaces: None, .. }` → any source is permitted (absent is distinct from empty — parity with CC's `null` check).
- `PluginPolicy { strict_known_marketplaces: Some(vec![]), .. }` → every source returns `PolicyRejection::NotInAllowlist`.
- `PluginPolicy { strict_known_marketplaces: Some([github("x/y")]), .. }` → only GitHub repo `x/y` (any ref, any path) is permitted; `github("x/z")` is rejected.
- Ref specificity: a rule with `git_ref: Some("main")` rejects a candidate with `git_ref: None` (cannot satisfy a concrete ref with "any ref" — policy.rs:312–318).
- The policy struct is loaded from a layered settings JSON chain; `managed: true` flag controls log level but not enforcement logic.
- This gate fires before any network or filesystem access in the install pipeline.

---

### B4 — `parse_skill_file` extracts YAML frontmatter + markdown body; invalid YAML returns `None` (not panic)

**CC reference:** `skills/loadSkillsDir.ts:447` calls `parseFrontmatter(content, skillFilePath)` from `utils/frontmatterParser.ts` which returns `{ frontmatter, content: markdownContent }`. CC uses a shared `parseFrontmatter` utility; it never panics on bad frontmatter — malformed YAML is surfaced via `logError` and the file is skipped (loadSkillsDir.ts:471–475 catch block).

**OC reference:** `src/skills.rs:37–55` `parse_skill_file`. Returns `Option<SkillDefinition>`:
- Returns `None` if the file cannot be read (`read_to_string` fails — `.ok()?`).
- Returns `None` if content does not start with `---` (no frontmatter delimiter).
- Returns `None` if the closing `---` is absent (`rest.find("---")?`).
- Returns `None` if `serde_yaml::from_str` fails on the YAML block (`.ok()?`).
- On success: `skill.prompt` = trimmed body after closing `---`; `skill.path` = the file path argument.

**Contract:**
- Input: `path: &Path` pointing to a `.md` file.
- **No frontmatter** (does not start with `---`): returns `None`.
- **Missing closing `---`**: returns `None`.
- **Valid frontmatter, valid YAML**: returns `Some(SkillDefinition)` with `name`, `description`, `allowed_tools`, `prompt`, `path` populated.
- **Valid frontmatter, invalid YAML** (e.g. `name: [unclosed`): returns `None` — **no panic** (serde_yaml parse error is silently dropped via `.ok()?`).
- **Unreadable file** (ENOENT, EPERM): returns `None` — no panic.
- Edge case: body is trimmed (`trim()`), so leading/trailing whitespace around the body is stripped (skills.rs:51).
- Edge case: `allowed_tools` is `Option<Vec<String>>`; if the YAML key is absent, defaults to `None` (serde default — skills.rs:25).
- **CC gap:** CC's `parseFrontmatter` supports many additional frontmatter keys (`when_to_use`, `argument-hint`, `model`, `effort`, `paths`, `user-invocable`, `hooks`, `shell`, `context`, `agent`, `disable-model-invocation`, `version`, `display-name` — loadSkillsDir.ts:185–265). OC parses only `name`, `description`, `allowed_tools`. (Gap filed: see #SKILL-FRONTMATTER-ISSUE.)

---

### B5 — `load_skills` scans project and user directories

**CC reference:** `skills/loadSkillsDir.ts:638–643` `getSkillDirCommands` loads from:
1. `<managedFilePath>/.claude/skills/` (policy/managed, only if `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_POLICY_SKILLS` is unset)
2. `~/.claude/skills/` (user scope, `userSettings`)
3. `<project-dir-and-parents-up-to-home>/.claude/skills/` (project scope, walked upward — `getProjectDirsUpToHome('skills', cwd)`)
4. `<--add-dir>/.claude/skills/` (additional dirs)
5. Legacy: `<project-and-parents>/.claude/commands/` (loaded as commands_DEPRECATED)

Skills from the `/skills/` directory only support the **directory format**: `<skill-name>/SKILL.md`. Plain `.md` files in `/skills/` are silently skipped (loadSkillsDir.ts:425–426 comment: "Single .md files are NOT supported in /skills/ directory").

**OC reference:** `src/skills.rs:59–119` `load_skills`. Scans two directories in order:
1. `.openclaudia/skills/` (project, relative path — CWD-based, not walked upward)
2. `~/.openclaudia/skills/` (user)

Supports both:
- Directory format: `<name>/SKILL.md` (skills.rs:82–96)
- Direct `.md` files in the skills dir (skills.rs:98–110)

**Contract:**
- Input: filesystem at `load_skills()` call time; no arguments.
- Both directories are scanned if they exist; missing directories are silently skipped (no error).
- For each directory: every immediate subdirectory is checked for `SKILL.md`; every `.md` file directly in the dir is also parsed.
- Files/directories that fail `parse_skill_file` are silently dropped.
- Name fallback: if the `name` field in YAML is empty string, the directory or file stem is used as the name (skills.rs:87–95, 101–108).
- **CC gaps:**
  - CC walks directories up to `$HOME` to find project skills; OC only looks in the CWD-relative `.openclaudia/skills/`. (Gap filed: see #SKILL-WALK-ISSUE.)
  - CC has a managed/policy skills layer (`<managedFilePath>/.claude/skills/`). OC has no equivalent. (Gap filed: see #SKILL-MANAGED-ISSUE.)
  - CC supports `--add-dir` additional skills directories. OC has no equivalent.
  - CC skips gitignored skill directories (loadSkillsDir.ts:892). OC does not.

---

### B6 — Skill name collision: project overrides user (precedence)

**CC reference:** `skills/loadSkillsDir.ts:717–763` assembles all skills in precedence order — managed → user → project → additional → legacy — then deduplicates by **resolved file path** (via `realpath`). A file loaded via two symlinks counts once. Name collision is not explicitly deduplicated by name: if two skills have the same name from different *files*, both survive and the model sees both (a known UX issue). Dedup is path-based, not name-based.

**OC reference:** `src/skills.rs:115–117`:
```rust
let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();
skills.retain(|s| seen.insert(s.name.clone()));
```
Skills are accumulated in directory-scan order: project dir first, then user dir. `retain` with `HashSet::insert` keeps the *first* occurrence for each name, so **project skills shadow user skills** with the same name.

**Contract:**
- Input: `load_skills()` called with both `.openclaudia/skills/` and `~/.openclaudia/skills/` containing a skill named `"foo"`.
- Expected output: the project skill (`foo`) is returned; the user skill is silently dropped.
- Precedence ordering: project directory is pushed to `dirs_to_scan` before user directory (skills.rs:64–75), so it sorts first in the Vec.
- If neither directory has a `foo` skill, no entry for `foo` is returned.
- **CC divergence:** CC deduplicates by *file path* (realpath), not by name. Two skills with the same name from different files both appear in CC's output. OC deduplicates by name, so only the first wins. This is an intentional divergence for OC simplicity — document it as an explicit policy difference. (Gap filed if CC name-based dedup is desired: see #SKILL-DEDUP-ISSUE.)
- Edge case: if a skill directory and a `.md` file in the same directory produce a skill named `"foo"`, the one encountered first in `read_dir` iteration order wins (filesystem-dependent ordering; no guaranteed stable order).

---

## Additional discovered gaps

The following behaviors exist in CC but are absent from OC. Each has been filed as a separate issue:

| Gap | Severity | CC source | OC status | Issue |
|-----|----------|-----------|-----------|-------|
| Plugin auto-update | high | `pluginAutoupdate.ts` — `autoUpdateMarketplacesAndPluginsInBackground()` refreshes marketplace git clones on startup, then calls `updatePluginOp` per plugin from auto-update-enabled marketplaces; result delivered via callback or stored as pending notification | Not implemented | #652 |
| MCP plugin integration weak | high (security) | `mcpPluginIntegration.ts:131–212` `loadPluginMcpServers` — reads `.mcp.json`, `manifest.mcpServers` (string/array/object), MCPB files; scopes server names as `plugin:<name>:<server>`; resolves `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`, `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}`, `${user_config.X}`, env vars; registers in ServiceRegistry | `mcp_configs` populated but **not wired into runtime ServiceRegistry** on plugin load | #654 |
| LSP plugin integration | high | `lspPluginIntegration.ts` — `loadPluginLspServers` reads `.lsp.json` and `manifest.lspServers`; validates path traversal; scopes as `plugin:<name>:<server>`; resolves env vars | Completely absent from OC | #655 |
| Plugin signature verification | high (security) | Signature check on plugin download/install | Not implemented | tracked under #521 |
| Zip-cache offline distribution | medium | `zipCache.ts` — plugins can be pre-packaged as `.zip` for offline install; `isPluginZipCacheEnabled()` gates the path | Not implemented | #656 |
| Skill frontmatter parity | medium | `loadSkillsDir.ts:185–265` — CC parses `when_to_use`, `argument-hint`, `model`, `effort`, `paths` (conditional skills), `user-invocable`, `hooks`, `shell`, `context`, `agent`, `disable-model-invocation`, `version`, `display-name` | OC parses only `name`, `description`, `allowed_tools` | #660 |
| Skill directory walk to home | medium | `getProjectDirsUpToHome('skills', cwd)` walks parent dirs up to `$HOME` | OC checks only CWD-relative `.openclaudia/skills/` | #661 |
| Managed/policy skill layer | medium | `<managedFilePath>/.claude/skills/` with `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_POLICY_SKILLS` env guard | Not implemented | #664 |
| Plugin delisting / auto-uninstall | medium | `pluginBlocklist.ts` `detectAndUninstallDelistedPlugins()` — at startup, compares installed plugins against marketplace manifest; auto-uninstalls delisted ones (where `forceRemoveDeletedPlugins: true`) and records them as flagged | Not implemented | #658 |
| Conditional skills (path-based activation) | medium | `loadSkillsDir.ts:820–1058` — skills with `paths:` frontmatter are held in a pending map; activated when the model touches a matching file | Not implemented | #665 |
| Skill name deduplication by path (CC) vs. by name (OC) | low | CC: dedup via `realpath` (path identity), allows same-named skills from different files. OC: first-name-wins via HashSet | Intentional divergence; document only | — |

## Done when

- This issue body has been edited to contain a markdown spec, one section per behavior above
- Each behavior section names the function(s) on both sides (or marks the OC side missing)
- Every gap above has been filed as its own issue and linked here
- The matching Phase 2 issue is unblocked

Blocked by: (none)
Blocking: #553
